─[ PBS Backup Troubleshooting ]─ ┐

name: pbs-backup-troubleshooting description: PBS and vzdump backup failure diagnosis and resolution user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob

PBS Backup Troubleshooting

Context

Diagnosing and fixing Proxmox Backup Server (vzdump, datastore, API) failures in a Proxmox environment.

Snags

  • Broken Pipe / Write Errors: Often caused by network instability or PBS datastore pressure.
  • API 5xx Errors: PBS service may be overloaded or the datastore is locked.
  • Space Exhaustion: Backups fail immediately if the datastore reaches the “full” threshold.

Workaround

  1. Check Space: df -h /var/lib/proxmox-backup/datastore on the PBS node.
  2. Review Logs:
    • Source node: /var/log/vzdump/*.log
    • PBS node: /var/log/proxmox-backup/tasks (use pct exec <vmid> -- ls ... if PBS is in a container).
  3. Manual Retry: 
    vzdump <vmid> --storage pbs-backups --mode snapshot --compress zstd

Safety Rules

  • Avoid deleting backups without operator confirmation.
  • Prefer expanding PBS storage or pruning old snapshots before aggressive deletion.
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
